description_long: This object appears to be a fragment of a decorative sword or ceremonial knife, featuring a long, flat blade with a highly ornate design. The blade is primarily white or light gray, with a repeating scroll or wave motif running along its length, suggesting classical or neoclassical influence. The surface shows signs of wear and some discoloration, possibly from age or exposure, and there are visible chips and irregularities along the edges, indicating it may be a relic or artifact. The handle area is not distinctly visible, but the overall form is elongated and thin, with the decorative band occupying a central position. The object is made predominantly of stone or plaster, rather than metal, which is unusual for functional blades and suggests a decorative or architectural purpose. The reverse side is less detailed, with the main focus on the ornamental band.
description: A long, thin decorative sword or knife fragment with a white body and a central ornate scroll motif, showing signs of age and wear.
description_view_0: A narrow side profile showing the thinness of the object and some edge irregularities.
description_view_1: A full view of the ornate scroll motif running along the length of the object, with visible surface wear and chipping.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile, again highlighting the object's thinness and some discoloration.
description_view_3: A mirrored view of the ornate motif, similar to view 1, with more visible surface texture and wear.
attribute_handle_design: not clearly visible; implied to be integrated with the blade, no distinct ergonomic features
attribute_ornamentation: repeating scroll motif along the blade
